content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN magic spells. The iii-th spell has a volatility of ViV_iViвЂ‹ and a strength of AiA_iAiвЂ‹.

When spells iii and jjj (1в‰¤i<jв‰¤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1в‰¤i<jв‰¤N) are activated together, they merge to form a single spell of strength (AiГ—Vj+ViГ—Aj)(A_i\times V_j + V_i\times A_j)(AiвЂ‹Г—VjвЂ‹+ViвЂ‹Г—AjвЂ‹).

What's the **maximum** possible strength of a spell Chef can obtain by activating exactly two of his spells?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of spells.
The next NNN lines describe the spells. The iii-th of them contains two space-separated integers ViV_iViвЂ‹ and AiA_iAiвЂ‹ вЂ” the volatility and strength of the iii-th spell.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible power of a combined spell.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are only two spells. Combining them results in a spell of strength 1Г—5+5Г—10=551\times 5 + 5\times 10 = 551Г—5+5Г—10=55.
Test case 222: It's optimal to choose the second and third spells. Combining them results in a spell of strength 8Г—2+10Г—9=1068\times 2 + 10\times 9 = 1068Г—2+10Г—9=106.
Test case 333: It's optimal to choose the first and fourth spells. Combining them results in a spell of strength 45Г—45+35Г—35=325045\times 45 + 35\times 35 = 325045Г—45+35Г—35=3250.
